title: Fall Risk
description: The individual is assessed as being at high risk of falls due to a combination of physical, cognitive, and environmental factors. Contributing factors may include reduced mobility, poor balance, muscle weakness, dizziness, impaired vision, or the use of mobility aids. The risk may be further increased by medical conditions, side effects of medication (such as drowsiness or low blood pressure), and episodes of confusion or reduced awareness of surroundings.
action_required: Immediate and ongoing actions are required to reduce the risk of falls and ensure the individual’s safety. Staff must closely monitor the individual at all times, particularly during mobility, transfers, and personal care activities. Assistance should be provided when standing, walking, or using stairs, and the individual should be encouraged to use prescribed mobility aids correctly at all times.

title: Self-Harm History - Significantly Improved
description: History of self-harm but has made exceptional progress - only very few incidents in over a year. Engages well with debriefs, reflections and chain analysis following any incidents. Currently receiving weekly CAMHS support with Nina, exploring EMDR and CBT therapies. January and February are particularly difficult periods emotionally.
action_required: Continue emotional support. Encourage CAMHS engagement. Complete keywork sessions on coping strategies. Provide extra support during January/February. Celebrate positive progress.

title: ADHD Medication Currently Paused
description: Elvanse 50mg ADHD medication currently on hold due to substance misuse concerns. Cannot be prescribed until YPDAAT sessions completed and no concerns of illegal substance use. Client understands the benefits but medication poses risk while substance concerns are present. Regular reviews with GP required.
action_required: Monitor for substance misuse concerns. Complete regular keywork sessions on substance education. Liaise with YPDAAT service and GP before medication can be reinstated.

title: School Attendance and Punctuality Issues
description: Struggles with school attendance and punctuality, particularly mornings. Now has 10-minute window when taxi arrives (no longer flexible). Missing important lessons affecting learning. Working under expectations in Year 11. Incentives in place to encourage attendance. Phone privileges linked to previous day's behavior at school.
action_required: Morning wake-up calls at 07:30 with tea. Prepare favorite breakfast (toast/hash browns, caramel iced latte). Help with hair/getting ready. Remind of incentives. If refuses, offer to arrange taxi and liaise with school. Complete revision/educational work daily to support Year 11 GCSEs.

title: Overdue Health Appointments
description: Overdue for dental check-up (rebooked for October 2025) and eye test (rebooked for August 2025). Has refused to attend appointments on the day. Team offering incentives to encourage attendance. Awaiting orthodontist appointment (very long wait times). On waiting list for dermatology referral (48-month wait) for skin reactions.
action_required: Continue rebooking appointments. Offer incentives. Complete keywork on importance of dental hygiene and health check-ups. Support attendance with encouragement and rewards. Chase orthodontist appointment.

title: Substance Misuse Concerns - Recent Incident July 2025
description: Recent incident regarding substance misuse in July 2025. Has completed YPDAAT program twice. Concerns regarding purchasing and taking substances with certain peers. Currently not supported to spend unsupervised time with specific friends (Liv, Sophie, Lilly, Maggie, Stevie, Mia, Ellie) due to these concerns. Team supporting with pocket money spending.
action_required: Continue keywork sessions on substance misuse dangers. Monitor peer relationships. Support attendance at GP appointments post-substance use. Educate on peer pressure and healthy choices. Contact Alex Fanning at YPDAAT for advice if needed: 07747564203

title: Vaping - Nicotine Reduction in Progress
description: Uses e-cigarette. Has declined smoking cessation services but is actively reducing nicotine levels. Switched from disposable vapes to refillable device. Successfully reduced from 20mg to 10mg nicotine. Positive progress - encourage continued reduction.
action_required: Continue keywork sessions on vaping risks. Celebrate progress with nicotine reduction. Continue to offer smoking cessation support. Goal: reduce to 0mg nicotine.

title: Medication Compliance Challenges
description: Struggles with consistent medication taking. Previously prescribed Folic Acid for low folate levels but medication returned to pharmacy due to refusal. Understands benefits but finds it difficult to maintain routine. Has prescribed medications for migraines (Zolmitriptan) and period pain (Mefenamic acid) to be taken as needed.
action_required: Complete keywork sessions on importance of medication adherence. Monitor and encourage Melatonin compliance. Offer support and reminders. Document any missed medications.

title: Healthy Lifestyle Support Needed
description: Can be selective with eating and prefers snacking on crisps and chocolate. Has sweet tooth. Struggles to engage in physical activities. New walking pad and fitness watch purchased to encourage home exercise. Gym membership available. Good progress with fruit snacking.
action_required: Involve in weekly menu planning. Encourage 5-a-day with favorite fruits/vegetables. Suggest fun activities: puppy yoga, swimming, walks, shopping trips. Use step counter challenge. Support small lifestyle changes like walking to shops.
